results.notes: Covered in T002-T003 with the sorting explanation, the 'world championship' metaphor, and the qualifier about specific test/run/prompt/rubric.
Covered in T004 with the MRI metaphor, the distinction between evaluating the artifact vs. the model, and the impossibility of testing all combinations.
T006 lists all components: Rails 8, RubyLLM, Hotwire, Tailwind, tests, CI, Dockerfile, Compose, documentation.
T006 lists all eight dimensions accurately.
T008 reproduces the full formula with all eight variables and the 'law of nature' caveat.
T010 covers non-determinism, same prompt not guaranteeing same outputs, and additional variables like terminal outputs, compaction, and provider alias updates.
T011 mentions Opus 4.7 with same prompt producing Tier 1 to Tier 3 spread across harnesses.
T012 reproduces both runs: 94/24min and 93/18min, different projects and defects.
T014 covers DeepSeek V4 Pro 69→89 and Kimi K2.7 86→68 with correct harness details.
T014 includes the caveats about snapshots/endpoints and the 'entire package' conclusion.
T018 covers the ballpark argument, insufficient sampling, and the 2.1% intelligence point.
T016 lists all seven entries with scores, times, and costs matching the original.
T020 covers ceiling effect, greenfield scope, saturation, and specific examples of point-deciding choices.
T022 defines Tier A (deploy as-is or short patch) and Tier C (broken core, major rework).
T024 covers 'choose the group first' and 'invented precision' phrasing.
T026 covers correct RubyLLM integration, real-signature test doubles, multi-worker safe session cookies, and error path coverage.
T028 covers cookie cap missing (4KB risk) and no API key preflight, plus the two-point deduction.
T030 covers singleton persistence, restart loss, multi-worker failure, and the one-point cost.
T030 includes the blind cross-audit observation about Rails.cache potentially flipping the order.
T032 gives all three models' token counts and per-million pricing accurately.
T032 states 15% fewer billable tokens and faster completion for 4.8 vs 4.7.
T034 mentions Anthropic describing 4.8 as efficiency-focused but does not explicitly state Anthropic 'maintained the price and highlighted tool calling efficiency' as distinct claims.
T034 covers Fable costing double per token without buying a better project, and 'too much model for this test.'
T034 covers the plateau hypothesis: Opus hit plateau at 4.7, 4.8 refined efficiency, Fable went to another tier the test couldn't extract.
T034 includes the caveat that this is a hypothesis, not insider knowledge about Anthropic's training.
T036 covers API key preflight, error differentiation, cache with TTL/cap, form object separation, and Dockerfile penalty.
T038 covers dev Dockerfile, no error path tests, no cookie byte limit, and drop to 85.
T040 covers Docker multi-stage non-root, 99.2% coverage, missing system prompt, hidden field history, reload loss and tamper risk.
T042 gives all three GPT models' token counts and costs accurately.
T044 provides the per-token pricing for 5.4 ($2.50/$15) and 5.5/5.6 ($5/$30).
T042 states ~23% faster and nearly half the tokens from 5.4 to 5.6.
T046 covers GPT 5.4 as the quality plateau jump, 5.5/5.6 refining efficiency.
T046 includes the caveat that he can't prove OpenAI's intent and labels it as his guess.
T048 covers the auditor as a variable with the key line: model didn't change, project didn't change, audit improved.
T048 covers GPT 5.5 dropping from 96 to 85 and GPT 5.4 from 97 to 95.
T050 covers auditing RubyLLM APIs from memory, marking valid APIs as hallucinations, and redoing the ranking.
T052 covers qualitative decisions squeezed into a number, blind spots, bad weightings, and misinterpretations.
T052 covers publishing prompt/code/logs/criteria/corrections and suspicion of graph-only publications.
T054 covers all three uses: eliminate bad groups, find candidate cluster, read specific defects.
T056 covers running your own test, swapping the prompt, multiple runs, blind review, measuring time/cost/human work.
T056 includes the Java monolith vs. Rails greenfield comparison.
T058 covers no absolute best LLM, task-dependency, and listing SWE-bench, Terminal-Bench, etc.
T060 covers clusters not podium, 90+ ballpark, Tier A vs C, and choosing by harness/subscription/time/defect.
T062 covers don't trust benchmarks/influencers/me, define constraints, write methodology, run candidates, bring data.
T054 covers irrelevance of cookie/Docker deductions vs. decisiveness of multi-turn/deploy deductions.
The script never mentions that the benchmark is open source or that Part 3 established the canonical methodology. T063 mentions the article title but not the benchmark's origin story.
T012 covers one-point difference as noise within the same model, supporting the insufficient-rodadas argument.

unsupported_claims: T063: 'stop being a fan of a model and start being a fan of your own testing process' — this specific formulation does not appear in the original content; the original says to define constraints, write methodology, and bring data, but never frames it as 'being a fan of your testing process'

T041: 'It's almost like they each have their own architectural taste' — the original does not attribute 'taste' or personality to models; this is an editorialized characterization by the script presenter
